Review the type changes made to InvoiceCashbackPoints models to ensure type consistency:

**File:** `src/screens/InvoiceCashbackPoints/models/index.ts`

**Changes:**
1. Added import: `import { PropsModalizeRef } from '@semparar/design-system-app/src/@types/modelize';`
2. Changed `items: []` to `items: CashbackInvoiceItem[]` in InvoiceCashbackViewProps
3. Changed `modalizeRef1: React.RefObject<React.Component>` to `React.RefObject<PropsModalizeRef>` in InvoiceCashbackViewProps

**Task:**
1. Read the models file and verify the types are correct
2. Check if `CashbackInvoiceItem` is properly defined in the same file
3. Verify the import path for `PropsModalizeRef` matches patterns used in other screens like:
   - `src/screens/Points/Models/index.ts`
   - `src/screens/InvoiceCashbackSummary/models/index.ts`
   - `src/screens/MyOrdersRewardProgram/models/index.ts`
4. Verify the changes don't break any existing type contracts

Let me also read the index.tsx file to see how the `modalizeRef1` and `items` are being used in the component logic.

file.content:
import {
  Box,
  Text,
  ListTextIcon,
  Button,
  InputBox,
  Icon,
  BackDropBottom,
} from '@semparar/design-system-app';
import React, { FC } from 'react';
import BasicScreen from '../../atomic/templates/BasicScreen';
import { InvoiceCashbackViewProps } from './models';
import { formatCurrencyBR, getFormattedPoints } from '../../utils/functions';
import { Skeleton } from '../../components/TransactionsListFactory/styles';

const InvoiceCashbackPointsView: FC<InvoiceCashbackViewProps> = ({
  handleGoBack,
  handleContinue,
  points,
  formattedPrice,
  handleChangeReais,
  handleChangePoints,
  handleUseAll,
  pointsValue,
  reaisValue,
  convertionValue,
  pointsError,
  reaisError,
  items,
  pointsQuantity,
  handleModalizeContinue,
  onOpenDefault,
  modalizeRef1,
  handleSelectEvent,
  height,
  width,
  isLoadingParameterization,
}) => {
  return (
    <BasicScreen
      hasScroll
      hasHeader
      bgColor="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_03"
      leftIconOnPress={handleGoBack}
      title="Cashback na fatura"
      hasRightIcon
      rightIcon="icon-help"
      rightIconOnPress={onOpenDefault}
      errorButtonOnPress={() => {}}
      isLoading={false}
    >
      <Box ml="smaller" mr="smaller" flex={1} justify="flex-start">
        <Text
          color="INTENSE_COLOR_05"
          testID="invoice-cashback-main-title"
          type="headingMDL"
          mt="smaller"
        >
          Defina quanto cashback quer na sua fatura
        </Text>

        <Box direction="row" mb="smaller" mt="nano" align="center">
          <Text type="paragraphMD" mr="quark">
            Pontos disponíveis:
          </Text>
          <Text type="paragraphXXXL" mr="quark">
            {getFormattedPoints(points || 0)}
          </Text>
          <Text type="paragraphMD">({formattedPrice})</Text>
        </Box>

        <InputBox
          label="Valor em pontos"
          placeholder="0"
          showLabel
          keyboardType="number-pad"
          value={pointsValue}
          onPressIn={() => handleSelectEvent('campo_pontos')}
          onChangeText={handleChangePoints}
          error={pointsError}
          textError="Você precisa informar um valor menor."
          textRight="Usar todos"
          onPressTextRight={handleUseAll}
        />

        <Box direction="row" mb="xxs" mt="xxs" align="center">
          <Box height="1px" bgColor="INTENSE_COLOR_02" width="45%" />
          <Icon
            name="icon-switch-vertical"
            size="xxs"
            color="INTENSE_COLOR_03"
            mr="nano"
            ml="nano"
          />
          <Box height="1px" bgColor="INTENSE_COLOR_02" width="45%" />
        </Box>

        <InputBox
          label="Valor em reais"
          placeholder="R$0,00"
          showLabel
          keyboardType="decimal-pad"
          onPressIn={() => handleSelectEvent('campo_valor')}
          value={reaisValue}
          onChangeText={handleChangeReais}
          error={reaisError}
          textError="Você precisa informar um valor menor."
        />
        {isLoadingParameterization && (
          <Box direction="row" mb="xxs" mt="xxs" align="center">
            <Skeleton width={width - 40} height={60} />
          </Box>
        )}
        {!isLoadingParameterization && (
          <Box direction="row" mb="xxs" mt="xxs" align="center">
            <Text type="paragraphMD">
              Cada {pointsQuantity || '0'} pontos valem {convertionValue || formatCurrencyBR(0)} de
              cashback na fatura.
            </Text>
          </Box>
        )}
      </Box>
      <Box ml="smaller" mr="smaller">
        <Button
          disabled={
            pointsValue === '' ||
            reaisValue === '' ||
            pointsValue === '0' ||
            reaisValue === '0' ||
            pointsError ||
            reaisError
          }
          text="Continuar"
          mt="large"
          mb="small"
          onPress={handleContinue}
          testID="invoice-cashback-button"
        />
      </Box>
      <BackDropBottom modalHeight={height * 0.53} modalizeRef={modalizeRef1}>
        <Text type="subtitleXXL" mb="smaller" mt="smaller" align="center">
          Use seus pontos para pagar menos na fatura
        </Text>

        <ListTextIcon items={items} ml="nano" mr="nano" type="list" mb="smaller" />

        <Button text="Entendi" onPress={handleModalizeContinue} />
      </BackDropBottom>
    </BasicScreen>
  );
};

export default InvoiceCashbackPointsView;

input.description: Run TypeScript compiler to check for type errors

content.text: There's a syntax error in the test file. Let me check it.

The test file has a syntax error - there's an unclosed `jest.mock` on line 19. But this is a separate issue from the type changes being reviewed. Let me focus on analyzing the type consistency of the models file changes.

## Type Review Summary

### Analysis of `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/screens/InvoiceCashbackPoints/models/index.ts`

#### 1. Import Path Verification for `PropsModalizeRef`

The import path `@semparar/design-system-app/src/@types/modelize` is **consistent** across all screens:

| File | Import Statement |
|------|------------------|
| **InvoiceCashbackPoints/models/index.ts** | `import { PropsModalizeRef } from '@semparar/design-system-app/src/@types/modelize';` |
| **Points/Models/index.ts** | `import { PropsModalizeRef } from '@semparar/design-system-app/src/@types/modelize';` |
| **InvoiceCashbackSummary/models/index.ts** | `import { PropsModalizeRef } from '@semparar/design-system-app/src/@types/modelize';` |
| **MyOrdersRewardProgram/models/index.ts** | `import { PropsModalizeRef } from "@semparar/design-system-app/src/@types/modelize";` |

All four screens use the exact same import path for `PropsModalizeRef`.

#### 2. `CashbackInvoiceItem` Type Definition

The `CashbackInvoiceItem` interface is **properly defined** in the same file (lines 38-45):

```typescript
export interface CashbackInvoiceItem {
  text?: string;
  type: TextIconType;
  icon: PropsIconsNameCore;
  iconColor: PropsTokenColorsCore;
  iconColorShape: PropsTokenColorsCore;
  description: string;
}
```

The `items` property in `InvoiceCashbackViewProps` is correctly typed as `CashbackInvoiceItem[]` (line 25).

#### 3. `modalizeRef1` Type Consistency

The type `React.RefObject<PropsModalizeRef>` is **consistent** with usage patterns:

- **In models**: `modalizeRef1: React.RefObject<PropsModalizeRef>;` (line 29)
- **In index.tsx**: `const modalizeRef1 = useRef<PropsModalizeRef>(null as unknown as PropsModalizeRef);` (line 200)
- **In view.tsx**: Passed to `BackDropBottom` component's `modalizeRef` prop (line 140)

Comparison with other screens:
- **Points/Models**: `modalizeRef: RefObject<PropsModalizeRef>;`
- **InvoiceCashbackSummary/models**: `modalizeRef1: React.RefObject<PropsModalizeRef>;`
- **MyOrdersRewardProgram/models**: `modalizeRefFilters: React.RefObject<PropsModalizeRef | null>`

The type is consistent with `InvoiceCashbackSummary` and follows the same pattern as `Points`.

#### 4. Type Contract Verification

The `InvoiceCashbackViewProps` interface is consumed by:
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/screens/InvoiceCashbackPoints/view.tsx` - Uses all typed props correctly
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/screens/InvoiceCashbackPoints/index.tsx` - Provides all required props with matching types

The `items` array is constructed in `index.tsx` (lines 137-145) with objects containing all required `CashbackInvoiceItem` properties.

### Conclusion

The type changes made to `InvoiceCashbackPoints/models/index.ts` are **correct and consistent**:

1. **Import path** for `PropsModalizeRef` matches all other screens in the codebase
2. **`CashbackInvoiceItem`** is properly defined in the same file and correctly referenced
3. **`modalizeRef1`** type is consistent with `InvoiceCashbackSummary` and standard React ref patterns
4. **No type contract breakages** - the view and index files correctly use the interface

The changes improve type safety by replacing:
- `items: []` (empty array literal type) with `items: CashbackInvoiceItem[]` (explicit typed array)
- `React.RefObject<React.Component>` (generic component ref) with `React.RefObject<PropsModalizeRef>` (specific modalize ref type)
